extraction.one_line_summary: HEALY Ben soloed to victory attacking 50km out, while ROGLIC gained 14 seconds on EVENEPOEL who showed weakness before the crucial TT
tactical_patterns: HEALY attacked first of two climbs at 50km out when breakaway companions expected traditional final-climb assault pattern.
ROGLIC counterattacked when EVENEPOEL faded, INEOS pairing THOMAS/GEOGHEGAN HART worked to amplify rather than chase gap down.
Breakaway formation required 80km of racing due to aggressive early pace, suggesting GC teams anticipated pre-TT attacks.

rider_signals.HEALY Ben: positive: Unprecedented willingness to attack ridiculously early (50km) in debut Grand Tour suggests exceptional confidence in sustained power output
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: First visible weakness before critical TT specialty stage reveals vulnerability to sustained climbing blocks before time efforts
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Opportunistically attacked when EVENEPOEL weak rather than saving energy before TT, prioritizing psychological pressure over fresh legs
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: INEOS dual-threat tactic with GEOGHEGAN HART amplifying ROGLIC move rather than passive following suggests coordinated anti-EVENEPOEL strategy
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: Mechanical/conflict with BAIS suggests team tension or equipment issues entering crucial ITT stage
